name: Acorn Squash and Apple Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Halve and seed the squash, cutting it with the cut side down on a rack over gently simmering water in a saucepan.
2. Cover and steam until tender, about 10 minutes.
3. Cool the squash slightly, then scoop out the pulp into a clean bowl.
4. In a heavy medium saucepan, combine 1/4 cup chicken stock, apples, and onion. Cover and cook over low heat for 10 minutes.
5. Add the squash pulp, remaining chicken stock, apple juice, ginger, and salt to the saucepan. Cover and simmer until all ingredients are very tender, about 20 minutes.
6. Puree the soup in batches using a processor or blender.
7. Strain the pureed soup through a sieve into a clean saucepan, pressing the puree with the back of a spoon.
8. Reheat the soup gently.
9. Add lemon juice and season with salt and a generous amount of pepper.
10. Serve the soup in bowls, garnished with yogurt and chives (or shredded fresh basil).
